message:
🐛 Fix 10 CNCF install missions with verified commands (#510)

Validated all missions against official documentation and fixed:

- eraser: Fixed version to v1.4.1, correct container image, added CRD step
- k8up: Fixed repo alias, added CRD install step, correct container image
- clusterpedia: Added --set installCRDs=true, removed fabricated steps
- piraeus-datastore: Complete rewrite with actual kubectl apply commands
- spiderpool: Fixed helm repo URL, removed destructive ns delete, fixed CRDs
- k8gb: Removed fabricated steps, fixed container image, added prereqs
- kubean: Complete rewrite with correct Helm install method
- oxia: Complete rewrite with correct Helm install method and port
- kubeelasti: Complete rewrite with OCI Helm install method
- hwameistor: Complete rewrite with Helm operator install method

Critical fix: Removed `kubectl delete namespace kube-system` from
spiderpool uninstall (would destroy entire cluster).

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -5,52 +5,65 @@
   "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
   "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
   "mission": {
-    "title": "Install and Configure Hwameistor on Kubernetes",
-    "description": "Hwameistor is an HA local storage system designed for cloud-native stateful workloads. It enables the creation of a local storage resource pool for managing various disk types, providing data persistence capabilities for applications.",
+    "title": "Install and Configure HwameiStor on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "HwameiStor is a Kubernetes-native HA local storage system for cloud-native stateful workloads. It creates a local storage resource pool from node disks and provides distributed data persistence through CSI volumes. The HwameiStor Operator deploys and manages all components automatically.",
     "type": "deploy",
     "status": "completed",
     "steps": [
       {
-        "title": "Create Namespace",
-        "description": "Create a dedicated namespace for Hwameistor to run in. Run the following command to create the namespace:"
+        "title": "Install node prerequisites",
+        "description": "HwameiStor requires LVM2 on all storage nodes. Install the required packages on each node:\n```bash\n# Debian/Ubuntu\napt-get install -y lvm2\n# RHEL/CentOS\nyum install -y lvm2\n```\nEach storage node must also have at least one unused (unpartitioned, unmounted) disk available."
       },
       {
-        "title": "Deploy Hwameistor Components",
-        "description": "Apply the necessary manifests to deploy Hwameistor components. Use the following commands:"
+        "title": "Add the HwameiStor Helm repository",
+        "description": "Add the HwameiStor Operator Helm chart repository:\n```bash\nhelm repo add hwameistor-operator https://hwameistor.io/hwameistor-operator\nhelm repo update\n```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Verify Installation",
-        "description": "Check if the Hwameistor pods are running correctly by executing the following command:"
+        "title": "Install HwameiStor using Helm",
+        "description": "Install the HwameiStor Operator, which will deploy all components:\n```bash\nhelm install hwameistor-operator hwameistor-operator/hwameistor-operator -n hwameistor --create-namespace\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the installation",
+        "description": "Check that all HwameiStor pods are running:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n hwameistor\n```\nYou should see the operator, local-storage, local-disk-manager, scheduler, admission-controller, and evictor pods."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify local disks are claimed",
+        "description": "Check that HwameiStor has detected the available disks on your nodes:\n```bash\nkubectl get localdisks\nkubectl get localdisknodes\n```\nAvailable disks should show as `Unclaimed` or `Claimed` depending on auto-claim settings."
       }
     ],
     "resolution": {
-      "summary": "A successful installation will have the Hwameistor pods running in the 'hwameistor' namespace, and you will see them listed when running `kubectl get pods --namespace hwameistor`. Additionally, the ServiceMonitor will be set up for monitoring.",
-      "codeSnippets": []
+      "summary": "A successful installation will show all HwameiStor pods running in the hwameistor namespace (operator, local-storage, local-disk-manager, scheduler, admission-controller, evictor). Local disks will be detected and StorageClasses will be available for PVC provisioning.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "helm repo add hwameistor-operator https://hwameistor.io/hwameistor-operator\nhelm repo update",
+        "helm install hwameistor-operator hwameistor-operator/hwameistor-operator -n hwameistor --create-namespace",
+        "kubectl get pods -n hwameistor",
+        "kubectl get localdisks"
+      ]
     },
     "uninstall": [
       {
-        "title": "Remove Hwameistor Components",
-        "description": "To uninstall Hwameistor, delete the deployed resources using the following commands:"
+        "title": "Remove HwameiStor",
+        "description": "First ensure no PVCs are using HwameiStor volumes, then uninstall:\n```bash\nhelm uninstall hwameistor-operator -n hwameistor\nkubectl get crd -o name | grep hwameistor.io | xargs kubectl delete\nkubectl delete namespace hwameistor\n```"
       }
     ],
     "upgrade": [
       {
-        "title": "Upgrade Hwameistor",
-        "description": "To upgrade Hwameistor, apply the new version manifests. First, delete the old version and then apply the new version:"
+        "title": "Upgrade HwameiStor",
+        "description": "Update the Helm repository and upgrade the operator:\n```bash\nhelm repo update\nhelm upgrade hwameistor-operator hwameistor-operator/hwameistor-operator -n hwameistor\nkubectl get pods -n hwameistor\n```"
       }
     ],
     "troubleshooting": [
       {
-        "title": "Pod Not Starting",
-        "description": "If the Hwameistor pods are not starting, check the pod logs for errors using:"
+        "title": "Local-disk-manager pods not starting",
+        "description": "The local-disk-manager runs as a DaemonSet and requires LVM2 on each node. Check if LVM2 is installed:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -n hwameistor -l app=hwameistor-local-disk-manager --tail=50\n```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Insufficient Resources",
-        "description": "If you encounter issues related to insufficient resources, ensure your cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated for the Hwameistor components."
+        "title": "No disks detected",
+        "description": "Verify that nodes have unused disks available (no partitions, not mounted):\n```bash\nkubectl get localdisks -o wide\nkubectl get localdisknodes\n```\nDisks that are already partitioned or mounted will not be detected."
       },
       {
-        "title": "CRD Issues",
-        "description": "If there are issues with Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs), verify that they are correctly applied by running:"
+        "title": "StorageClass not available",
+        "description": "Check that the operator has created the default StorageClasses:\n```bash\nkubectl get sc | grep hwameistor\nkubectl describe cluster.hwameistor.io\n```"
       }
     ]
   },

+        "description": "Create a sample GSLB resource to manage your services globally. Note: K8gb requires a custom values.yaml with your DNS zone, edge DNS servers, and cluster geo tags configured.
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: k8gb.absa.oss/v1beta1
kind: Gslb
metadata:
  name: hello-world-gslb
  namespace: default

      app: hello-world

    type: failover
    primaryGeoTag: "eu"
    dnsTtlSeconds: 30
EOF
```"

@@ -5,56 +5,52 @@
   "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
   "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
   "mission": {
-    "title": "Install and Configure Kubeelasti on Kubernetes",
-    "description": "KubeElasti is a Kubernetes-native solution that enables scale-to-zero functionality, allowing services to scale down during periods of low or no traffic without losing requests. Installing KubeElasti helps optimize resource usage and reduce costs while maintaining service availability.",
+    "title": "Install and Configure Elasti (KubeElasti) on Kubernetes",
+    "description": "Elasti is a Kubernetes-native scale-to-zero solution that allows services to scale down during periods of low or no traffic without losing requests. It uses a lightweight resolver proxy to hold incoming requests while scaled-down workloads are brought back up, optimizing resource usage and reducing costs.",
     "type": "deploy",
     "status": "completed",
     "steps": [
       {
-        "title": "Create KubeElasti Namespace",
-        "description": "Create a dedicated namespace for KubeElasti to run in. Run the following command to create the namespace:\n```bash\nkubectl create namespace kubeelasti\n```"
+        "title": "Install Elasti using Helm (OCI)",
+        "description": "Install Elasti from the OCI Helm registry into a dedicated namespace:\n```bash\nhelm install elasti oci://tfy.jfrog.io/tfy-helm/elasti --namespace elasti --create-namespace\n```"
       },
       {
-        "title": "Deploy KubeElasti Components",
-        "description": "Apply the KubeElasti manifests to deploy the components. Use the following commands:\n```bash\nkubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/truefoundry/KubeElasti/v1.0.0/deploy/kubeelasti-controller.yaml -n kubeelasti\nkubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/truefoundry/KubeElasti/v1.0.0/deploy/kubeelasti-webhook.yaml -n kubeelasti\n```"
+        "title": "Verify the installation",
+        "description": "Check that the Elasti operator and resolver pods are running:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n elasti\n```\nYou should see the `elasti-operator` and `elasti-resolver` pods in a Running state."
       },
       {
-        "title": "Verify Installation",
-        "description": "Check if the KubeElasti pods are running successfully:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n kubeelasti\n```"
+        "title": "Verify CRDs",
+        "description": "Confirm that the Elasti Custom Resource Definitions are registered:\n```bash\nkubectl get crd | grep elasti\n```\nYou should see `elastiservices.elasti.truefoundry.com`."
       }
     ],
     "resolution": {
-      "summary": "A successful installation of KubeElasti will show the pods running in the `kubeelasti` namespace. You can further verify that the service is operational by checking the logs or accessing the service endpoints as needed.",
+      "summary": "A successful installation will show the elasti-operator and elasti-resolver pods running in the elasti namespace. The ElastiService CRD will be available for configuring scale-to-zero behavior on your workloads.",
       "codeSnippets": [
-        "kubectl create namespace kubeelasti",
-        "kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/truefoundry/KubeElasti/v1.0.0/deploy/kubeelasti-controller.yaml -n kubeelasti\nkubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/truefoundry/KubeElasti/v1.0.0/deploy/kubeelasti-webhook.yaml -n kubeelasti",

+        "helm install elasti oci://tfy.jfrog.io/tfy-helm/elasti --namespace elasti --create-namespace",
+        "kubectl get pods -n elasti",
+        "kubectl get crd | grep elasti"
       ]
     },
     "uninstall": [

-        "title": "Remove KubeElasti",

+        "title": "Remove ElastiService resources and Helm release",
+        "description": "Delete any ElastiService custom resources first, then uninstall the Helm release:\n```bash\nkubectl delete elastiservices.elasti.truefoundry.com --all --all-namespaces\nhelm uninstall elasti -n elasti\nkubectl delete namespace elasti\n```"
       }
     ],
     "upgrade": [
       {
-        "title": "Upgrade KubeElasti",
-        "description": "To upgrade KubeElasti, apply the new manifests from the latest release.
